name: azure-appservice-domain-tls-redirect-ops
description: Operate custom domains, TLS bindings, and host-based redirects on Azure App Service (Linux), including hostname inventory, cert gap detection, and nginx redirect validation.

name: azure-automation-setup
description: Set up Azure Automation accounts, runbooks, schedules, and identity/RBAC for scheduled ops tasks; use for lessons learned and repeatable setup of Automation + Storage + DNS access.

name: azure-cli-fastpath
description: Speed up and de-flake Azure CLI (az) investigations with safe PowerShell wrappers for auth/subscription checks, JSON-first output, extension install avoidance of interactive prompts, resource ID resolution, and canonical JSON comparison. Use when you’re repeatedly running az queries or troubleshooting quoting/extension/MFA issues on Windows.

name: azure-deploy-method-decision
description: Decide whether to deploy by direct Azure Files share upload (hotfix path) or full CI/CD pipeline run. Use for App Service deployments where speed vs safety tradeoffs must be explicit.

name: azure-php-linux-appservice-ops
description: Operational setup and troubleshooting for PHP on Azure App Service (Linux), including nginx docroot overrides, appCommandLine/startup scripts, Kudu vs runtime container differences, log collection (startup.log, az webapp log download), and PHP extension/deprecation issues (e.g., mcrypt on PHP 8.x). Use when deploying or debugging PHP apps on Azure App Service (Linux).

name: azure-vm-to-app-service-migration
description: Plan and execute migration of workloads from an Azure VM to Azure App Service. Use for VM-to-App-Service projects, migrations, modernization cases, or Tier 3 cases that require discovery, decision-making, staging, cutover, rollback, and stakeholder validation.

name: develop-web-game
description: Use when Codex is building or iterating on a web game (HTML/JS) and needs a reliable development + testing loop: implement small changes, run a Playwright-based test script with short input bursts and intentional pauses, inspect screenshots/text, and review console errors with render_game_to_text.

name: doc
description: Use when the task involves reading, creating, or editing `.docx` documents, especially when formatting or layout fidelity matters; prefer `python-docx` plus the bundled `scripts/render_docx.py` for visual checks.

name: es-file-lookup
description: Use when the user asks to find or locate files by name or path on Windows (e.g., "find file X", "where is Y", "search for *.csproj"). Always use the Everything CLI (es.exe) instead of Get-ChildItem/dir/PowerShell recursion unless es.exe is missing or errors.

name: fast-file-io-selection
description: Select the fastest file-reading method in PowerShell/.NET based on intent (raw bytes, full text, or streaming text). Use when performance matters, when comparing Get-Content vs .NET APIs, or when choosing file IO for large or medium files.

name: fortigate-change-window-runbook
description: Execute FortiGate change windows with safe sequencing, explicit validation checkpoints, rollback triggers, and evidence logging for audit-ready operations.

name: fortigate-log-evidence-normalizer
description: Parse and normalize FortiGate/FortiCloud/FortiAnalyzer VPN log exports into evidence-ready summaries, including event distributions, success/failure patterns, and schema-gap checks required for control validation.

name: fortigate-plink-key-bootstrap
description: Add or update FortiGate admin SSH public keys for PuTTY/plink, enable SSH on the correct FortiGate interface, and verify non-interactive key login with pinned host key fingerprints.

name: fortigate-vpn-hardening-execution
description: Execute a phased FortiGate VPN hardening plan with feasibility gates, low-risk sequencing, validation checkpoints, rollback notes, and evidence collection for CMMC/SOC-aligned operations.

name: mcp-git-npx-packaging-readiness
description: Validate and harden an MCP package so it runs from git-based npx installs (bin/prepare/build/runtime compatibility).

name: mcp-openapi-full-parity-server
description: Build a full-capability MCP server from OpenAPI specs with deterministic tool naming, mutation safety gating, committed generation artifacts, and parity verification.

name: mcp-tool-catalog-docgen
description: Generate and maintain an MCP tool catalog from a manifest, including compatibility tools, workflow tools, examples, and safety contracts.

name: nexport-accessibility-wcag-2-1-enforcer
description: NexPort skill: enforce WCAG 2.1 AA expectations on new or changed UI, with concrete review criteria for forms, async feedback, overlays, focus behavior, and responsive flows.

name: nexport-ajaxq-request-guarding
description: NexPort skill: when and how to use ajaxq + beforeSend guards to prevent duplicate AJAX requests.

name: nexport-api-swagger-docs
description: Add and validate Swagger-facing XML docs and example payloads for NexPort Web APIs.

name: nexport-appsec-pre-commit-gate
description: Run a multi-stack application security review on staged changes before commit (.NET, Python, PHP, Node.js), including dependency vulnerabilities, code scanning, and secrets checks.

name: nexport-azure-appservice-log-triage
description: Diagnose Azure App Service startup and runtime failures by pulling Kudu, App Service, and application logs with Azure-authenticated workflows. Use when a deployment succeeds but the site is unhealthy, returning 5xx, or failing during startup.

name: nexport-azure-pipeline-compiler-validation
description: Validate Azure Pipelines YAML with Azure DevOps' actual pipeline compiler and resource checks. Use when editing pipeline YAML, diagnosing template expansion or service-connection authorization failures, or verifying variable groups, service endpoints, and pipeline definitions before queueing runs.

name: nexport-azure-pipeline-status

description: Logging, telemetry, and auditing guidance for NexPort. Use when discussing logging config, Serilog/log4net usage, audit logs, app logs, telemetry events, observability dashboards, or deciding where to record a system/user event.

description: Provision, validate, authorize, and cut over NexPort Managed DevOps Pools safely.

name: nexport-memory-hygiene

description: Apply the NexPort NHibernate AAAA session pattern for controller and service tests: fresh session/transaction per phase, explicit initial asserts, and deterministic multi-step flow setup.

description: Create NexPort Campus release announcements in https://github.com/NexPort-Solutions/NexPort-Campus-Documentation, including drafting the release note, adding the file under getting-started/whats-new, and linking it in SUMMARY.md and getting-started/whats-new/README.md. Use for new releases based on FogBugz cases and deployment date.

name: nexport-roadmap-hygiene
description: Keep roadmaps, plans, and task lists aligned with actual progress; prevent redundant churn by verifying status before starting work and updating status after completion.

name: nexport-smoke-test-runner
description: Run NexPort smoke tests with a consistent prompt that always asks whether UI or unit smoke tests are desired. In Linux terminals, Roslyn MCP StartTest/TestSolution is preferred; in PowerShell terminals, use native Windows dotnet tooling first.

name: nexport-solr-aks-replication
description: Replicate NexPort Solr deployment on AKS using automation-first az/kubectl workflows. AKS-only; no Tomcat path.

name: nexport-t4-resource-generation
description: Run NexPort localization T4 templates explicitly and verify generated resource outputs are current.

name: nexport-tenant-preview-simulation
description: Implement strict tenant-view simulation toggles for internal workspace users (request-scoped preview, effective access resolver, preview UX cues, and required tests).

name: nexport-test-db-restore-preflight
description: Preflight NexPort.Tests database restore prerequisites before unit or integration test runs. Use when running NexPort.Tests locally, when SQL restore/setup fails, or when the repo is under a user-profile or Codex worktree path that SQL Server may not be able to read.

description: NexPort skill: nexport-test-logging-diagnostics.

description: Parse and summarize TRX test results, extract failures fast, and benchmark TRX parsers (pygixml/ElementTree/xunitparserx/rg). Use when asked to analyze large TRX outputs or compare parsing speed/correctness.

name: nexport-ui-fixtures-my-courses
description: UiTests fixtures for My Courses/My Training coverage (seeding data, login-as, selectors, and common pitfalls).

name: nexport-ui-tests-runner
description: Run NexPort Playwright UI tests end-to-end (start/stop site, build, run tests, check logs). Use when asked to run UiTests/Playwright tests, start a UiTests site for tests, or troubleshoot UI test timeouts (always review logs first). In Linux terminals, Roslyn MCP is preferred for build/start/test; in PowerShell terminals, use native Windows dotnet tooling first.

name: nexport-ui-tests-startup-troubleshooting
description: Start/stop and troubleshoot NexPort Playwright UI tests in UiTests env. Use when the app or UiTests endpoints return 404/503, when persona login fails, or when dashboard tools never load during UI tests.

name: nexport-ui-ux-checklist-enforcer
description: NexPort skill: review a change set against docs/ui-ux/checklist.md and produce a concrete pass/fail report with file-specific fixes.

name: nexport-ui-visual-walkthrough
description: Run a manual UI visual walkthrough/check. In Linux terminals, build/start with roslyn_code_navigator first; in PowerShell terminals, use native Windows dotnet tooling first; then validate pages/interactions with Playwright MCP when available.

name: nexport-user-docs
description: Update NexPort Campus end user documentation in https://github.com/NexPort-Solutions/NexPort-Campus-Documentation, including release announcements, SUMMARY.md, and What's New. Use when asked to add/modify user docs or align docs with a NexPort Campus release branch; handles branch selection rules, scratch checkout, and optional branch mapping updates.

name: nexport-vocabulary-glossary-enforcement
description: NexPort skill: detect user-facing terminology drift and engineer-centric jargon; propose fixes and glossary updates aligned to docs/ui-ux/glossary.md.

name: nexport-web-host-preflight
description: Preflight NexPort web-host startup for browser/UI work. Use before UiTests, Playwright, manual browser validation, or any task that depends on local HTTP/HTTPS hosting, certificates, launch profiles, and ignored appsettings files.

name: nexport-wordpress-blog-publisher
description: Create or update NexPort Solutions blog posts on https://nexportsolutions.com using the WordPress REST API with Application Passwords. Use when publishing or revising Elementor-backed blog posts that use the Theme layout, when selecting categories and tags for NexPort announcements, or when generating a featured image for a website post with OpenAI image generation.

name: pdf
description: Use when tasks involve reading, creating, or reviewing PDF files where rendering and layout matter; prefer visual checks by rendering pages (Poppler) and use Python tools such as `reportlab`, `pdfplumber`, and `pypdf` for generation and extraction.

name: php-lint-checks
description: PHP syntax linting helpers (php -l) and workflows for validating changed files or selected paths. Use when asked to verify PHP syntax, lint modified files, or add quick PHP lint scripts.

name: qa-ready-to-testing
description: Use when a FogBugz case is ready for QA validation. Trigger on requests like "ready for QA", "send to QA", "move to testing", or "resolve this case". The required action is to set the case status to Resolved (To Testing), not Beta or Production.

name: research-orchestration
description: Research workflow for when a question requires investigation or validation. Use to coordinate local repo context + Quick Memory, Microsoft Learn docs MCP (for Microsoft/Azure topics), Context7 MCP (for programming libraries), and web searches for up-to-date or niche info.

name: sensitive-artifact-scan
description: Scan project artifacts for sensitive material (for example keys, PSKs, tokens, secrets) and produce remediation actions such as redaction, rotation, and storage-control follow-ups.

name: spreadsheet
description: Use when tasks involve creating, editing, analyzing, or formatting spreadsheets (`.xlsx`, `.csv`, `.tsv`) with formula-aware workflows, cached recalculation, and visual review.

name: sysops-project-kickoff
description: End-to-end workflow for SysOps projects/cases (kickoff, planning, execution, validation, progress updates). Use for starting or maintaining SysOps projects, updating plans/phases, tracking progress, or organizing case work across the full lifecycle.

name: test-creation-lessons
description: Repo-specific guidance for creating or updating tests in fogbugz-mcp and similar MCP TypeScript repos. Use when adding coverage for payload shaping, envelope contracts, safety gates, or workflow tools without network calls.

name: update-progress
description: Sync completed session work into durable progress artifacts. Use when asked to update progress, capture session progress, wrap up a session, write a stakeholder update, post a case update, sync status, create a handoff note, sync roadmap or plan checkboxes, create a quick-memory progress note, or post a progress report to the active FogBugz case, GitHub issue, or related tracker.

name: windows-network-admin-preflight
description: Validate and prepare a Windows admin workstation network path before infrastructure changes, including adapter selection, static IP/DNS setup, route/metric checks, and rollback-to-DHCP steps.
